name: Hillman Financial Services
description_short: Atlanta-based financial services firm offering tax preparation, credit coaching, and financial coaching led by CEO Bianca D. Hillman, a Certified Financial Coach.
description_long:
Hillman Financial Services is an Atlanta-based consulting firm founded in 2015 by Bianca D. Hillman, who launched the company after experiencing tax fraud and identity theft. Hillman previously worked as an Insurance and Claim Analyst and completed apprenticeships in accounting and tax preparation while earning certifications in property and casualty underwriting. She holds a Bachelor's degree in Business Management from Clayton State University and is a member of the Credit Consultants Association and a Certified Financial Coach through Womanly Wealth Academy.

The firm offers a range of financial services including tax preparation and planning for individuals and businesses, credit coaching, financial coaching and budgeting assistance, bookkeeping services, and life insurance referrals. They emphasize personal relationships with clients and operate a client portal for tax preparation management. Services are available via consultation bookings and virtual meetings (Zoom calls mentioned in testimonials).

Hillman Financial Services distinguishes itself through personalized, one-on-one service with the founder directly involved in client relationships. The firm targets individuals with limited financial literacy and those intimidated by tax or credit processes. Testimonials highlight Bianca's patience, knowledge, and ability to work with clients at their current skill level, including those unfamiliar with QuickBooks or tax forms. Long-term client relationships are emphasized, with one client citing business since 2016.

As a smaller consulting firm rather than a large financial institution, the company operates on a free consultation basis and appears to charge for services on an individual project fee basis rather than offering standardized products. The website lacks transparent pricing information, and the breadth of services offered (spanning tax, credit, insurance, and bookkeeping) suggests a generalist approach rather than specialization in any single area.
pros: Founder Bianca D. Hillman is a Certified Financial Coach through Womanly Wealth Academy and member of Credit Consultants Association
Offers free consultation to assess financial needs before engaging services
Personalized one-on-one service with founder directly involved rather than delegated to junior staff
Testimonials demonstrate long-term client relationships (one client since 2016)
Explicitly addresses clients with low financial literacy and those intimidated by financial processes
Virtual meeting capability (Zoom calls) provides flexibility for remote clients
Founded specifically to protect consumers from fraud and identity theft after founder's personal experience
Client testimonials cite quick turnaround, patience, and detailed attention to complex tax and bookkeeping matters
cons: No transparent pricing information available on website; must book consultation to learn costs
Very small firm (appears to be founder-operated) may have limited capacity or availability
Broad service offerings (tax, credit, insurance, bookkeeping) suggest generalist approach rather than deep specialization
Limited information about credentials: no mention of CPA, Enrolled Agent, or IRS certifications for tax preparation
No published credentials verification, reviews on third-party sites, or Better Business Bureau information provided
best_for: Small business owners preparing business taxes for the first time and needing patient guidance
Individuals with limited financial literacy seeking personalized help with budgeting and credit improvement
Atlanta-area residents preferring local, relationship-based financial consulting over large corporate firms
Consumers who have experienced or fear fraud/identity theft and need a trusted advisor
diagnosis: Hillman Financial Services is best for Atlanta-area individuals and small business owners seeking personalized, judgment-free financial guidance from a knowledgeable advisor with lived experience in fraud prevention. The main caveat is the lack of transparent pricing, limited firm capacity due to founder-operated model, and absence of documented professional credentials (CPA/Enrolled Agent status) that would be standard for tax preparers.
services: Credit coaching and credit score improvement
Client portal access for tax preparation management
Identity theft and fraud protection guidance
QuickBooks training and accounting software support
